candidates.title: How-To Tuesday: Sew a Stuffie
candidates.description: Elementary-age kids are invited to join us each Tuesday at 1:00 to learn a little bit about a fun skill or hobby that maybe they haven’t tried before! We have experts from the community here to teach you the basics! Come make your own tiny stuffed animal using felt cutouts and sewing supplies! Learn how to adhere buttons, embroider details, stitch together and stuff some felt animal shapes and more! This project may be tricky for the younger elementary grades, so please have adult supervision for assisting in threading and knots! 周二跟我学: 缝制毛绒玩具 每周二一点我们邀请小学同学们一起学习尝试一种全新的技能或者兴趣爱好。我们有来自社区的专家教你入门！ 一起来用毛毡片亲手制作属于自己的迷你毛绒玩具吧！我们将学习如何缝钉纽扣、刺绣细节、缝合及填充毛毡动物等技能。低年级小学生需由成人陪同，协助完成缝针和打结等步骤。 ¡Niños en edad escolar primaria están invitados a unirse a nosotros cada martes a la 1:00 para aprender un poco sobre una habilidad o pasatiempo divertido que quizás no hayan probado antes! ¡Tenemos expertos de la comunidad aquí para enseñarte los conceptos básicos!

candidates.title: Iowa City Math Circle Summer Camp - Math 2
candidates.description: The Iowa City Math Circle has been enriching students for 8 years. We are holding a free Math Camp for middle schoolers and high schoolers interested in preparing for math competitions, specifically AMC and AIME, as well as math beyond the school curriculum. We recommend a prerequisite of at least Algebra 1 to understand the material. Camp Schedule: Jul 6 - Jul 10: Math 1 Jul 13 - Jul 17: Math 2 Jul 20 - Jul 24: Coding 1 Jul 27 - Jul 31: Coding 2 Registration is required. Go to Iowa City Math Circle to register or if you have any questions. Snacks will be provided during every camp session. Join our instructors for lots of fun and learning this summer! Sponsored by the Iowa City Math Circle and Iowa City Public Library Teen Services.

The ICPL Book Bike will also be on site and ready to engage with families as they explore nature and learning together! candidates.title: Flower Painting w/ Austina Scott
candidates.description: Intended for adults & young adults (16+), registration is required. As a Summer Reading treat, we've gotten a special guest, Austina Scott, to give us a painting tutorial. Artist Austina Scott will teach us how to paint. Everyone will paint a picture of yellow flowers against a background of sky teaching us the process. We'll provide the canvases, paints, and paintbrushes. This program is free to attend. Please contact us for access needs. Ph: 319-624-2678 | Email: staff@solon.lib.ia.us

candidates.title: Let's Talk Books: Fathers & Daughters
candidates.description: Relationships between fathers & daughters can run the spectrum from deeply connected and heartwarming to frought with issues at every turn. Fiction about fathers & daughters can be found in every genre - from general fiction to mysery, science fiction, or even westerns. Gather a few of your favorites and join us for the disscussion. Goodread's Fathers & Daughters list or Father Daughter Relationship lists or an ICPL catalog search for can give lost of inspiration. Not a traditional book group, Let's Talk Books is a series of casual online conversations about books. We meet on the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month on Zoom to talk about our favorite books in a specific genre or on a theme that changes each time. We each bring a few titles to share, and by the end of the discussion we all have a few new titles on our to-be-read lists. Register here and you'll receive a confirmation email with the Zoom login details. You'll also get a reminder email a few days before the event. Everyone is welcome!

candidates.title: Veterans For Peace chapter 161 meeting

candidates.title: Stories in the Park at Wetherby Park
candidates.description: Join us for Stories in the Park all summer long! Note the early Stories in the Park time at 9:30 to beat the midmorning heat! We will offer the same Storytime over the course of the week, so please choose one park location a week to attend. We will be at Willow Creek park on Mondays, Wetherby Park on Wednesdays, Mercer Park on Fridays. Wednesday are in Wetherby Park, at 2398 Taylor Drive, Iowa City. The ICPL Bookmobile will be on site and open for checkout. Library cards can be issued and updated. In the event of rain, the program will be canceled but the Bookmobile will still be on site. 暑期公园故事会将于每周一，三，五上午9：30 举行，周一的活动地点在Willow Creek 公园，周三在 Wetherby 公园，周五在 Mercer 公园。不同地点每周的活动内容基本一样，请选择一个方便的公园参加。 周三的故事会将在Wetherby公园举行，地址是2398 Taylor Drive, Iowa City。 ICPL 图书车也将来到现场为您提供开卡和借书服务。如遇雨天，故事会将取消，图书车仍照常服务。 ¡Acompáñanos a Cuentos en el Parque durante todo el verano! ¡Ten en cuenta el horario temprano de Cuentos en el Parque a las 9:30 a. m. para evitar el calor de media mañana! Presentaremos la misma hora de cuentos durante toda la semana, así que te pedimos que elijas solo una ubicación por semana para asistir. Estaremos en Willow Creek Park los lunes, en Wetherby Park los miércoles y en Mercer Park los viernes. Los miércoles estaremos en Wetherby Park, ubicado en 2398 Taylor Drive, Iowa City. El bibliobús de ICPL estará presente y disponible para realizar préstamos. También se podrán emitir y actualizar tarjetas de la biblioteca. En caso de lluvia, el programa se cancelará, pero el bi

candidates.title: Chair Yoga
candidates.description: What is chair yoga? Chair yoga is a beneficial form of yoga for any fitness level, from active seniors to those recovering from an injury. Chair yoga has the following benefits for older adults: low impact on joints, improves flexibility, and stress reduction. Certified instructor Julie Ellen will lead a FREE hour-long class for Adults every Wednesday at 10 AM, following Coffee & Conversations. The attached PDF waiver must be completed prior to your first participation in Chair Yoga and updated annually. Copies are available at the Library or you may print ahead. Please contact us for access needs. | Ph: 319-624-2678 |Email: staff@solon.lib.ia.us

candidates.title: Author Visit Storytime with Charlotte Gunnufson: Hard Hat Hank, with Habitat for Humanity
candidates.description: Come meet author Charlotte Gunnufson and listen to her read her book, "Hard Hat Hank and the Sky-High Solution." Enjoy a fun, construction-themed Storytime, and then stick around for some construction crafts, including an activity with the Iowa Valley Habitat for Humanity for their Frame-A-Thon, in which they invite community members (kids included!) to decorate 2x4s that will be used to frame a new home. Join us in the Storytime Room for Summer Storytime on Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday and Saturday mornings at 10:30am. The Storytime librarian will vary each week, and every day is open to all ages! Catch the all-ages Thrilling Thursday programs 10:00am in Meeting Room A. This is additional Storytime option to Stories in the Park at 9:30am on Mondays at Willow Creek Park, Wednesdays at Wetherby Park, and Fridays at Mercer Park! 暑期故事会将于每周一，二， 三，五, 六上午10：30在图书馆儿童活动室举行。 每周将由不同的工作人员带来不同的活动内容，欢迎所有年龄段的朋友参与！每周四上午10:00 将在会议室A举办Thrilling Thursday周四特别活动。 此外，图书馆还将于每周一，三，五上午9：30举办公园故事会，周一上午活动地点在 Willow Creek Park，周三在 Wetherby Park， 周五在 Mercer Park。 今日特别活动：儿童作家夏洛特·甘努夫森（Charlotte Gunnufson）见面会。作者将分享自己的童书作品《戴安全帽的汉克与“冲上云霄”的解决方案》（*Hard Hat Hank and the Sky-High Solution*），并带来建筑主题的手工活动。 Únete a nosotros en la Sala de Cuentos para la Hora del Cuento de Verano los lunes, martes, miércoles y viernes por la mañana a las 10:30 am. ¡La bibliotecaria de la Hora del Cuento variará cada semana y todos los días están abiertos para todas las edades! ¡No te pierdas los emocionantes programas para tod

candidates.title: Help Hours in the Digital Media Lab
candidates.description: Want some help starting a digital media project like scanning slides, editing a video, or converting a VHS tape? Expect to have questions while you use Adobe software? During Help Hours, a staff member can help you get started with a project, problem-solve struggles with our software and hardware, or just show you what's available in the Digital Media Lab. Individuals interested in the Senior Planet Computer Essentials class that was canceled may also drop in to work through the information at their own pace, with Stacey available for one-on-one instruction whenever demand allows. The Cricut, Elgato digital converters, and 8mm film converter can be reserved during this session. Go to icpl.org/DML to check availability and make your reservation. The inkjet printer is also available for drop-in printing. Note: Ordinary tech questions about using your devices or navigating a website can be asked anytime at the 2nd floor Info Desk.

candidates.title: World Wednesday: A Taste of Pisa!
candidates.description: Plant a seed, in every beautiful corner of the world! This summer, school age children and their families are invited to join our weekly World Wednesday program from 6/10 to 8/5. Each week we will go on an adventure to a different part of the world through craft, performance, or activity. This is the perfect opportunity to explore and celebrate the diverse world cultures! Come join us for a taste of Pisa! Yes, you read that right—it's not a typo! Pisa is a city in Italy famous for its iconic Leaning Tower, which has been tilting for over 800 years. Make your own mini version of the tower, then snap a fun photo on our green screen with beautiful Italian scenery—just like visiting Italy! 种下一粒种子，在世界上每一个美丽的角落！暑假期间我们诚邀学龄儿童及家人参加6月10日至8月5日每周三举办的“世界星期三”活动。每期活动中我们将通过手工、表演等各种活动来到不同的国家和地区开启一段探索之旅，共同了解和庆祝多元的世界文化！ 今天来和我们一起体验“比萨”风情！比萨（Pisa）是意大利的一座名城，以其标志性的“比萨斜塔”而闻名于世——这座塔倾斜屹立已逾八百年。我们将自己动手制作一座迷你斜塔模型，并可在绿幕前拍摄趣味照片，就像真的亲临意大利一般！

candidates.description: Chris That Twists is a storyteller and professional balloon twister making his debut at the Iowa City Public Library! Join us for his "Twisty Tales", interactive storytelling with balloons, balloon tricks and more! This interactive balloon show will be sure to lift spirits of all ages! Join us for a Thrilling Thursday program at 10am or 1pm every Thursday in the summer! 气球先生 Chris That Twists 即将亮相爱荷华城公共图书馆！欢迎参加今天的“气球奇趣故事会”（Twisty Tales），一场融合了讲故事，气球造型，魔术表演等精彩互动内容的活动，一定能让各个年龄段的观众都乐在其中！ 暑假期间每周四上午 10 点和下午 1 点将举办“精彩星期四”系列活动！

candidates.description: Learn about setting healthy boundaries with people, how to tell if someone is gaslighting you, acting in a way that is toxic, and learn to have constructive and meaningful conversations with family and friends. This program is facilitated by the University of Iowa's Violence Prevention Team.

candidates.title: Totally Tweens: Under-the-Sea Sensory Jars
candidates.description: It's Disability Pride Month! Join us to learn the amazing story of the disabled daredevil who made a splash in sports history. Charles "Zimmy the Human Fish" Zibelman, whose legs were amputated after a childhood accident, became a record-setting endurance swimmer in midcentury New York - and his lively personality was as much a draw for the public as his feats in the water. After hearing a new picture book biography of Zimmy's life, make an "under-the-sea" sensory jar that you can take home and think of Zimmy whenever you use it! This event is for kids in grades 3-6. 六月是“残障人士自豪月”！和我们一起来了解一位在体育史上大放异彩的残障特技游泳健将查尔斯·齐贝尔曼（Charles Zibelman），又名“人鱼齐米”（Zimmy the Human Fish）。他在童年时期因遭遇事故而截肢，后于20世纪中叶在纽约成为了一名屡破纪录的耐力游泳运动员；他那充满活力的个性和水上运动的惊人成就深深吸引着公众。我们将聆听关于齐米生平的全新传记绘本故事，动手制作一个“海底世界”主题的感官瓶带回家。

candidates.title: Create your own Summer Journal with ICPL pt 4: writing prompts
candidates.description: Are you curious about journals and journaling? Do Art journals, bullet journals, or writing journals interest your? Want to explore the differences? Join us for a series called "Creating Your Own Summer Journal" as part of the Adult Summer Reading Program to explore a variety of fun ways you can create and use your own journal. We will meet the 1st and 3rd Thursday June - August. At the 4th meeting we're going to explore writing in our journals with fun writing prompts. If you didn't attend the first meeting and get a kit, or you already have a journal of your own you can bring that to work on. Or a blank notebook works too. Registration is not required, but suggested if you want an emailed reminder of this event

candidates.title: Party in the Park at Mercer Park
candidates.description: Gather with neighbors and friends during the annual Party in the Park series! From June 11 through August 13, weekly on Thursdays from 6:30-8:00pm, enjoy live music, visits from the ICPL Bookmobile, and outdoor recreation activities at some of your favorite Iowa City Parks. More details can be found on the City of Iowa City webpage .

candidates.title: Ear Buds: an audiobook walk
candidates.description: Load an audiobook on your device, put on your walking shoes, and join us for a walk in the area around Mercer Park! Most of the time will be spent walking silently among others while you listen to your own audiobook on headphones. Each of us will stroll at our own comfortable pace. At the beginning and end of the event, the headphones will come off and you’re encouraged to chat with others about what you’re reading and swap recommendations. We will meet in the entryway of the Mercer Park Aquatic Center and Scanlon Gym. Our walk will go around the park, across Lower Muscatine, up to the Court Hill Trail, along the creek, and past Heyn's Ice Cream (for a cold treat on us!) on our way back to Mercer. This paved route is approximately 1.75 miles. At the end of the loop, we'll rest with conversation in the ICPL Bookmobile (at Mercer 9:15-10:15am). The Bookmobile has a full browsing collection of books and movies available for checkout on board. Bring any questions to our library staff on the Bookmobile or just stop in to explore and learn. New to digital audiobooks? No problem! Visit the library or Bookmobile before our walk for support accessing Libby or Hoopla with your library card. Whether you’re an introvert or extrovert, we think you’ll find something to enjoy in this outing! Staff: Stacey. Cosponsored with Iowa City Parks & Recreation. Registration is optional if you'd like to receive an email reminder. Note: In the case of inclement weather, the event will be canceled and

candidates.title: Open from 9 AM to 2 PM
candidates.description: We're open from 9 AM to 2 PM due to Solon Beef Days.

candidates.title: Member Hour
candidates.venue: Iowa Children’s Museum, 1451 Coral Ridge Ave., Coralville, IA, 52241, United States
candidates.description: Members of The Iowa Children’s Museum are invited to come play when The ICM is ONLY open to members! Member Hour is 9-10 a.m. every Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, and Saturday. Come play early and enjoy a less busy environment! Learn more about membership here.

candidates.title: Stories in the Park at Mercer Park
candidates.description: Join us for Stories in the Park all summer long! Note the early Stories in the Park time at 9:30 to beat the midmorning heat! We will offer the same Storytime over the course of the week, so please choose one park location a week to attend. We will be at Willow Creek park on Mondays, Wetherby Park on Wednesdays, Mercer Park on Fridays. Fridays are in Mercer Park, near the playground equipment. The ICPL bookmobile will be on site and open for checkout. Library cards can be issued and updated. In the event of rain, the program will be canceled but the bookmobile will still be on site. 暑期公园故事会将于每周一，三，五上午9：30 举行，周一的活动地点在Willow Creek 公园，周三在 Wetherby 公园，周五在 Mercer 公园。不同地点每周的活动内容基本一样，请选择一个方便的公园参加。 周五的故事会将在Mercer公园的游乐场旁边举行。 ICPL 图书车也将来到现场为您提供开卡和借书服务。如遇雨天，故事会将取消，图书车仍照常服务。

candidates.title: Summer Storytime
candidates.description: Join us in the Storytime Room for Summer Storytime on Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday and Saturday mornings at 10:30am. The Storytime librarian will vary each week, and every day is open to all ages! Catch the all-ages Thrilling Thursday programs 10:00am in Meeting Room A. This is additional Storytime option to Stories in the Park at 9:30am on Mondays at Willow Creek Park, Wednesdays at Wetherby Park, and Fridays at Mercer Park! 暑期故事会将于每周一，二， 三，五, 六上午10：30在图书馆儿童活动室举行。 每周将由不同的工作人员带来不同的活动内容，欢迎所有年龄段的朋友参与！每周四上午10:00 将在会议室A举办Thrilling Thursday周四特别活动。 此外，图书馆还将于每周一，三，五上午9：30举办公园故事会，周一上午活动地点在 Willow Creek Park，周三在 Wetherby Park， 周五在 Mercer Park。 Únete a nosotros en la Sala de Cuentos para la Hora del Cuento de Verano los lunes, martes, miércoles y viernes por la mañana a las 10:30 am. ¡La bibliotecaria de la Hora del Cuento variará cada semana y todos los días están abiertos para todas las edades! ¡No te pierdas los emocionantes programas para todas las edades los jueves en un nuevo horario, a las 10:00 am en la Sala de Reuniones A! ¡Esta es una opción adicional para la Hora del Cuento en el Parque a las 9:30 am los lunes en el Parque Willow Creek, los miércoles en el Parque Wetherby y los viernes en el Parque Mercer!

candidates.title: Toddler Storytime
candidates.description: Storytime geared toward 2-3yo

candidates.title: Iowa City Math Circle Summer Camp - Math 2
candidates.description: The Iowa City Math Circle has been enriching students for 8 years. We are holding a free Math Camp for middle schoolers and high schoolers interested in preparing for math competitions, specifically AMC and AIME, as well as math beyond the school curriculum. We recommend a prerequisite of at least Algebra 1 to understand the material. Camp Schedule: Jul 6 - Jul 10: Math 1 Jul 13 - Jul 17: Math 2 Jul 20 - Jul 24: Coding 1 Jul 27 - Jul 31: Coding 2 Registration is required. Go to Iowa City Math Circle to register or if you have any questions. Snacks will be provided during every camp session. Join our instructors for lots of fun and learning this summer! Sponsored by the Iowa City Math Circle and Iowa City Public Library Teen Services.

candidates.title: Teen Summer: Johnson County Conservation Raptor Program
candidates.description: YES interns and staff will be on hand to share live raptors and answer your questions. The Johnson County Conservation Department strives to provide county residents access to the highest quality healthy natural areas, outdoor recreation opportunities and conservation education. These services are provided at more than 18 sites throughout Johnson County including natural areas, parks, preserves, and trails. The Conservation Department headquarters are located at the 1,062 acre F.W. Kent Park, with additional operations centers located at Cangleska Wakan, Pechman Creek Delta, and Sutliff.

candidates.title: Friday Flick: Encanto
candidates.description: Take a break from the summer sun and join us every Friday for a matinee movie showing! Retells the story of the Madrigals, an extraordinary family that lives in the mountains of Colombia in a charmed region called Encanto-a wondrous place whose magic becomes threatened and whose rescue depends on the only member of the Madrigal family who doesn't possess magical gifts. 周五影院：《魔法满屋》

candidates.title: Stories Between Generations: Letter-Writing Event
candidates.description: ***FOR KIDS ALREADY REGISTERED FOR THE "STORIES BETWEEN GENERATIONS" BOOK CLUB/PEN PAL PROGRAM.*** Join us in the Storytime Room from 4-5 PM to write to your pen pal! We will provide templates with prompts and fun writing utensils, as well as some individual support with composing your letter. When you're done, leave your letter here and we'll get it to your pen pal at the senior center! Registration for this program occurred May 1-15. This event is only for kids in grades 3-8 already enrolled in the in-progress Stories Between Generations program.

candidates.title: Teen Summer: Mystery Movie Night
candidates.description: Sometimes we need choices in life. There will be five movie offerings and we can decide as a group what is going on the big screen. Popcorn will be provided.

candidates.title: ICPL Bookmobile at Iowa City Farmers Market
candidates.description: Iowa City Public Library Bookmobile will be popping up at the Iowa City Farmers Market monthly from 8:00 am to 11:00 am this summer. Various activities will be offered, making this a fun Farmers Market stop for all. Iowa City Farmers Market is located at the Chauncey Swan parking ramp at 405 E Washington Street. The Bookmobile will be open for browsing and holds pick up with your ICPL library card. Don’t have a card? You can sign up for a Library card on the Bookmobile when you bring your photo ID with an example of your current address. The ICPL Bookmobile is wheelchair accessible.

candidates.description: Join us on Saturday, July 18, from 9:00 a.m. to noon for a special space-themed celebration at The Iowa Children’s Museum! In partnership with NASA and the Iowa Space Grant Consortium, we’ll explore exciting missions and discoveries through hands-on activities in our party room. Guests can learn about NASA’s work and experience the fun of space science in action. Activities included with museum admission. Additional details coming soon—mark your calendar and get ready for some out-of-this-world fun!

candidates.title: William Morris, Beatrix Potter, and the World of Victorian England
candidates.description: Join the University of Iowa Libraries’ Special Collections and Archives, in partnership with The Beatrix Potter Society and Iowa City Public Library, as we host a full day of events celebrating Beatrix Potter’s 160th birthday. This recorded talk with professor emerita and founder of the William Morris Archive Florence Boos as she discusses the intersections of William Morris and the life of Beatrix Potter in the evolving world of Victorian England.

candidates.title: Operation: Body Exploration with the University of Iowa (2nd - 6th Grades Only)
candidates.venue: 311 Marengo Road, Tiffin, IA, United States, Iowa 52340
candidates.description: Sat, 18 Jul at 10:00 CDT

candidates.title: The Songs of Peter Rabbit
candidates.description: Enjoy a special interactive storytelling of the Beatrix Potter's Tale of Peter Rabbit, featuring the Songs of Peter Rabbit by Dudley Glass and a full reading of the story. Come by to see and hear Peter Rabbit like never before with movement, live music and crafts to follow the story! On July 18th, 2026, The University of Iowa Special Collections & Archives, in partnership with The Beatrix Potter Society, will be hosting a full-day series of events to celebrate 160 years of Beatrix Potter. Enjoy an open house of rare Beatrix Potter and other children’s literature materials housed within the archives, an interactive presentation with a Beatrix Potter impersonator, a tea party, and an academic panel. 欢迎参加别开生面的互动式彼得兔故事会！活动将完整朗读Beatrix Potter笔下的《彼得兔的故事》，并教唱Dudley Glass创作的《彼得兔之歌》，通过肢体动作、现场音乐以及手工制作等环节，带您体验彼得兔的世界！ 2026年7月18日，爱荷华大学（University of Iowa）特藏与档案馆将携手碧翠丝·波特协会（The Beatrix Potter Society）举办一系列全天活动，以纪念碧翠丝·波特诞辰160周年。届时，您将有机会参观档案馆珍藏的碧翠丝·波特作品及其他儿童文学文献，欣赏由扮演者呈现的碧翠丝·波特互动展示，并参加茶会及学术研讨会。

candidates.title: ICPL Summer Puzzle Palooza: A Team Puzzle Competition
candidates.description: Are you a dissectologist? Do you love jigsaw puzzles? Join ICPL's Summer Puzzle Palooza event for adults. Puzzle Palooza is a Team Puzzle Competiton made of 12 four-person teams, who will have 120 minutes to assemble the same 500-piece puzzle. The first team to finish wins. If at the end of 120 minutes no one has completed the puzzle the team with the fewest free pieces will be declared the winner. Members of the winning team will receive a new puzzle as a prize. Team Pre-registration is required and will begin on June 2. There is a limit of 12 four-person teams. Please have only one member of your team register. This event is part of the ICPL Adult Summer Reading Program.

candidates.title: Socially Anxious Board Games
candidates.description: Are you socially anxious, introverted, new in town, or just looking for an easy way to meet new people? Come join our Socially Anxious Hangout Group to play some games and have a bit of fun! (Knowing how to play games is not required.) Our group on MeetUp.com is for adults in the Iowa City-Cedar Rapids corridor looking to meet new people, and we host regular events to play various board, card, and party games. Have a specific game that you'd like to play? Feel free to bring it along, as our hosts have a limited game selection. Interested in more? Find us on MeetUp at http://www.meetup.com/meetup-group-xzwuwaiu

Our friends from the Coralville Public Library will also join us with the Book Bike. Enjoy some free, family activities as we learn through play! ICM2Go dates: June 22nd (with Toadally Awesome Pets) July 6th July 20th (with Iowa Worm Farm) August 3rd (with the Coralville Fire Department) You can also find us at a variety of outreach events throughout the summer months!

candidates.title: Chair Yoga
candidates.description: What is chair yoga? Chair yoga is a beneficial form of yoga for any fitness level, from active seniors to those recovering from an injury. Chair yoga has the following benefits for older adults: low impact on joints, improves flexibility, and stress reduction. Certified instructor Julie Ellen will lead a FREE hour-long class every Monday at 12:30 PM. The attached PDF waiver must be completed prior to your first participation in Chair Yoga and updated annually. Copies are available at the Library or you may print ahead. Please contact us for access needs. | Ph: 319-624-2678 |Email: staff@solon.lib.ia.us

✨makes me want a hot dog realllll bad✨ Prepare yourself, next week is National Hot Dog Day (July 15th). We rounded up some hot dog hot spots in the Iowa City area just for you. #thinkiowacity #iowacity #hotdog

Some early numbers on a 𝐁𝐚𝐧𝐚𝐧𝐚𝐬 4th of July weekend 🍌🍌 ➡️ Johnson County recorded 279,000 total visitors from outside the county. ➡️ Kinnick Stadium had 176,400 visitors last weekend, of which 88% traveled from outside Johnson County. Read more about the impact events like this have on Johnson County at link in bio. 📸 📸 @josephwcress | @thinkiowacity
Vibes. Always with @core4.bike Bikes. Beats. One of the best gravel parties in the biz… 🎧 DJ lineup: • PhDJ • @wolf1200 • @sdiekie_ • @theartofansel 🔥

Think Like a Local episode two: Calyx Creek Lavender and Lodging Lavender season is here, and this is a summer stop you’ll want to add to your list. Stroll through the lavender fields, browse the shop for handcrafted lavender goods, grab a drink or a bite from the café, and take a peek at their brand new treehouses for a truly unique stay. Located right outside Iowa City, this special place is a must-stop. 💜 #ThinkLikeALocal #ThinkIowaCity @calyxcreek

From locals to first-time visitors, here’s what Banana Ball fans love most about Iowa City. What did they miss? Let us know your favorite Iowa City spot in the comments. 👇 #ThinkIowaCity #IowaCity #SavannahBananas
🎆🎶♥️ @summerofthearts 📸: #ThinkIowaCity

ON STANDS THIS WEEK — 25TH ANNIVERSARY ISSUE 🎉 "Had enough of Mega-this, Super-that and Big-whatever? Fed up with being treated like a dumbass by your local corporate newspaper? Say hello to the first issue of Little Village, your recipe for relief in 32 pages of words and pictures," reads a note from publisher @toddkimm55 in the July 2001 magazine. "Little Village favors the small and local, champions the sustainable, yet believes in considering more than one side of an issue ... Our goal is to start fires and put them out, to raise expectations and lower the limbo bar, to be serious and to be stupid. If you don't like one thing, you might like another." The more things change here at Little Village — longer issues, statewide coverage, a salaried staff of editors — the more things stay the same. We're still in print, still free, still local, ad-supported, independent, serious and stupid. In this issue: Kimm and other former LV publishers and editors reflect on a quarter century and 354 issues. Plus: Longtime LV music columnist @kembrew recalls his favorite interviews for Prairie Pop over the decades. Also: Ketamine therapy clinics are cropping up in Iowa as a unique UI study utilizing the drug gets underway, and owners of small venues are in agreement with audiences: concert ticket prices are too damn high.

Calling all writers! The Des Moines Public Library is again partnering with Little Village magazine to host a short story competition! All stories must be submitted by August 31 at 12 PM. Learn more about the contest and get started writing: (Link in bio)

LV's most enduring columnist recalls his favorite story to research, some of the icons he's interviewed, and the hot tub party that inspired the title "Prairie Pop." Read the retrospective at the link in our bio, and sign up for LV newsletters to get stories like these delivered directly to your inbox.⁠ ⁠ 🖋️ @kembrew⁠ 📸 Kembrew McLeod from a photo by Tom Jorgenson; collage by @kdoolittleart

Large concentrated hog farms that send manure contamination into local waterways not only impact human health but threaten the existence of more than a dozen endangered species, according to a new study published in the journal Conservation Science and Practice. Read the story at the link in our bio, and sign up for LV newsletters to get the news delivered directly to your inbox.⁠ ⁠ 🖋️ Brian Bienkowski/The New Lede⁠ 📸 USGS

While Central Iowa Water Works put more resources than ever into their Nitrate Removal Facility last year, other suppliers of drinking water around the state failed to meet EPA standards. Read the story at the link in our bio, and sign up for LV newsletters to get the news delivered directly to your inbox. ⁠ ⁠ 🖋️ Cami Koons/Iowa Capital Dispatch⁠ 📸 Zak Neumann & @emmamcclatch; collage by @kdoolittleart
The latest issue of @littlevillagemag. See you at One of Us this September!

Buzz over to the University of Iowa's "living laboratory" on Saturday and help researchers spot species for the sake of science. More details at the link in our bio 🐝⁠ ⁠ 🖋️ Paul Brennan⁠ 📸 Courtesy of APLL (@sees_ui)

The Live Nation/Ticketmaster monopoly facilitates price-gouging, reseller scams and the dreaded "radius clause." Nevertheless, Iowa's indie stages persist.⁠ ⁠ “I think I can speak for other live small venues that support original music: it’s kind of a calling,” said Dave Deibler, owner of @octopuscollegehill. Read the story at the link in our bio, and sign up for LV newsletters to get the news delivered directly to your inbox.⁠ ⁠ 🖋️ @emrosa22⁠ 🎨 Live Nation headquarters in Beverly Hills, California, photo by Coolcaesar, CC BY 4.0; Independent venue illustrations by @lanirosedesigns; Collage by @kdoolittleart

Iowa may soon be back to swing-state status, according to ISU political science professor Karen Kedrowski. Read the story at the link in our bio, and sign up for LV newsletters to get the news delivered directly to your inbox.⁠ ⁠ 📸 @emmamclatch⁠ 🖋️ Kadin Luhmann/Iowa Capital Dispatch

This Sunday, the Iowa City Aerohawks (and guest pilots) will show off their tricks at their 33rd Annual Air Show. This may be your last chance to pull up a lawn chair and watch them at their current field, adjacent to the Iowa City Landfill just off IWV road, where they’ve taken to the skies since 1972. Rich DeVepo, who spoke with LV ahead of the event and for a story in LV’s April’s mag, is proud to be part of a club that invites all phases and walks of life, whether truck drivers or rocket scientists, emphasizing that all ages and genders may find interest in soaring to new heights.⁠ ⁠ DeVepo notes that while some people unexpectedly join the club on a drive to the landfill, drawn in by the sight of planes diving and twisting overhead, the Air Show is an excellent way to discover your interest in the hobby.⁠ ⁠ If you would like to bring a pop-up canopy for sun cover, DeVepo recommends getting there early. Otherwise, bleachers will be available, though you may need to bring a lawn chair, as the Aerohawks have welcomed as many as 2,000 attendees in previous years.⁠ ⁠ Find the full story at the link in our bio ✈️⁠ ⁠ 🖋️ William Lowell⁠ 📹️ @yeagernc

"That’s the strange alchemy of building something with the right people, in the right place, at the right time: you make choices and take risks, and then serendipity keeps handing you the collaborators and the moments that make those choices and risks pay off," said Alissa Hansen, former LV publisher. Read the retrospective at the link in our bio, and sign up for LV newsletters to get stories like these delivered directly to your inbox.⁠
Futile Wrath — July 2026 🍕⁠ ⁠ 🖋️ @sam_locke_ward

Mark your calendars for this week's Music on the Move! Join us this coming Wednesday, July 15, for an incredible evening of live music with the Goat Hollow Boys. They'll be bringing their high-energy, original Iowa Country-Punk sound to the park, and it's a show you won't want to miss! Bring your lawn chairs, blankets, and snacks for a wonderful, family-friendly evening of local music in the community. A huge thank you to Hills Bank, our wonderful presenting sponsor, for making free community events like this possible! When: Wednesday, July 15 | 6:30 PM – 8:00 PM | Where: Happy Hollow Park (800 Brown St, Iowa City, IA) | Cost: FREE! #sotaic26 #sotaic #SummerOfTheArts #MusicOnTheMove #IowaCity #LiveLocalMusic #HillsBank #CommunityArts #FreeEvents #GoatHollowBoys

The weather is beautiful and the music is going to be electric tonight! Grab your blankets and lawn chairs and meet us at Riverfront Crossings Park for Rhythms at Riverfront Crossings featuring PhDJ AND Afroband243! Come celebrate community, culture, and high-energy Congolese Rhumba beats starting at 7:00 PM. We have everything you need for the perfect night out: - Beverage Garden: Stop by our Summer of the Arts tent to grab a cold beer or drink! - Dinner is Served: Snag some delicious local eats from Luna's Tacos, MooMoo's, and Marco's Grilled Cheese. - Activities & Tabling: Get to know local groups! Stop by the tables for P&G to see what they have to share. - Cost: FREE admission! We want to send a huge shoutout and thank you to our fantastic evening co-sponsors, McDonald Optical and Accurate Automotive, Inc., for helping us keep these community concerts entirely free for everyone. See you all at the park tonight! #sotaic26 #sotiac #IowaCityArts #SummerOfTheArts #RiverfrontCrossings #Afroband243 #PhDJ #McDonaldOptical #AccurateAutomotive #FreeLiveMusic #SupportLocalArts #ICFoodTrucks

Found yourself dancing a little too hard and misplaced something? Don’t worry, we’ve got your back! We collected all items left behind at the 2026 Iowa City Jazz Festival. Slide into our DMs or reach out to our team at the Summer of the Arts office to see if we have your missing gear so we can get it back to you! #sotaic26 #sotaic #IowaCityJazz #LostAndFound #FestivalTips #DowntownIowaCity #SummerOfTheArts

TONIGHT IN THE PED MALL! The weekend is officially here, and the weather is perfect for some live music! Join us tonight at the Ped Mall Stage from 6:30 PM – 9:00 PM for an unforgettable performance by Winterland! Come down and experience their soulful tribute to the Grateful Dead. From rhythm and blues roots to experimental psychedelic rock, they capture an incredible era of music with absolute perfection. Every face in the crowd will be smiling—don't miss out on the magic tonight! Time: 6:30 PM – 9:00 PM | Location: Ped Mall Stage (Downtown Iowa City) | Cost: FREE admission! We want to give a special shoutout and thank you to our amazing sponsor, Graduate by Hilton Iowa City, for partnering with us to keep these Friday night concerts free for everyone. See you all downtown tonight! #sotaic26 #sotaic #IowaCityArts #SummerOfTheArts #FridayNightConcertSeries #WinterlandBand #GraduateByHilton #DowntownIC #FreeLiveMusic #SupportLocalArts

MARK YOUR CALENDARS FOR SATURDAY! We have an incredible weekend lineup waiting for you! This Saturday, July 11th, join us at Riverfront Crossings Park from 7:00 PM – 9:30 PM for an unforgettable night of live music featuring PhDJ AND Afroband243! Come experience the amazing rhythms of Afro and Congolese Rhumba music. We have a full evening of fun planned beyond the music: - Beverage Garden: Stop by our tent and grab a cold beer or refreshing drink! - Food Vendors: Skip cooking and grab dinner from Luna's Tacos, MooMoo's, and Marco's Grilled Cheese! A massive thank you to our wonderful Series Presenting Sponsor, Kim Schillig, Realtor at Lepic-Kroeger, Realtors, for her incredible support in bringing these free concert series to our community! #IowaCity #SummerOfTheArts #RhythmsAtRiverfrontCrossings #Afroband243 #PhDJ #KimSchilligRealtor #FreeEventsIC #LiveLocalMusic #IowaCityParks #LocalFoodTrucks

Skip cooking tonight and join us for Eat for the Arts at 30hop ! Whether you're grabbing lunch, meeting up for happy hour, or enjoying dinner on the rooftop patio, 15% of your total sales ALL DAY TODAY will be generously donated back to Summer of the Arts. Your meals and drinks directly help us keep our community arts programming free for everyone to enjoy! When: Today! 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM Where: 30HOP Coralville (900 E 2nd Ave) Dine-in and takeout both count! See you on the rooftop! #sotaic26 #IowaCity #CoralvilleIA #SummerOfTheArts #30hop #EatForTheArts #DineOutDoGood #SupportLocalIC

Ready to travel back in time to the golden era of rock? Mark your calendars for this Friday, July 10th, because Winterland is taking over the Ped Mall Stage for our Friday Night Concert Series! Specializing in the Grateful Dead's legendary catalog from 1969 through the late '70s, Winterland brings an uncanny authenticity, incredible musicianship, and a beautiful, high-energy connection to the crowd. Bring your friends, grab your lawn chairs, and get ready to dance the night away! A massive thank you to our wonderful presenting sponsor, McGrath Toyota of Iowa City, and our incredible stage co-sponsors, Bread Garden Market & Bakery, ReUnion Brewery, and Joseph's Steakhouse Iowa City, for supporting free live music in our community! #sotaic #sotaic26 #IowaCity #SummerOfTheArts #FridayNightConcertSeries #Winterland #GratefulDead #GraduateIowaCity #FreeEventsIC #DowntownIowaCity

TONIGHT IS THE NIGHT! We are heading to the park tonight! Grab your family, friends, and lawn chairs and meet us at the Terry Trueblood Recreation Area for Music on the Move featuring Hard Luck & the Troubles! Come out and enjoy their soulful, gritty Americana and blues-driven energy right here in Iowa City, fronted by the incredible lead vocals and piano of Roland Racevskis. It's the perfect way to spend a beautiful Wednesday summer evening! Time: 6:30 PM – 8:00 PM | Location: Terry Trueblood Recreation Area | Cost: FREE! We want to extend a special thank you to our wonderful band sponsor, @Navigate Homes, for supporting tonight's performance and helping keep the arts thriving in our community! See you all tonight! #IowaCity #SummerOfTheArts #MusicOnTheMove #HardLuckAndTheTroubles #NavigateHomes #IowaCityArts #FreeLiveMusic

Join us on July 9 for Eat for the Arts at 30hop Coralville! Whether you’re stopping in for lunch, grabbing a spot on the rooftop patio, or enjoying a family dinner, 15% of total sales for the day will be donated to Summer of the Arts. Your meal helps support free, family-friendly arts programming across our community! 30 HOP is a local favorite known for: - Modern Industrial Vibe & Rooftop Patio: The perfect atmosphere to relax with great views, excellent cocktails, and friendly service. - American Bites with an Asian Fusion Twist: A fantastic, unique menu with something for everyone. - Incredible Craft Beer Selection: Dozens of draft lines featuring the best local and regional brews. Eat well. Give back. Keep the arts thriving in our community! #sotaic26 #sotaic #30hopCoralville #IowaFoodie #EatForTheArts #SupportTheArts
